summary: I was born in South Korea in 1993, and I spent part of my childhood there. In 2002, my family moved to Gyeonggi Province, where I faced challenges adjusting to a new school and making friends. Although I could speak Korean, I struggled with schoolwork compared to my classmates. Living in South Korea helped me build my Korean language skills and identity.

My family moved to Canada in 2007 during the immigration process to the United States. We lived in London, Ontario, and stayed there for about a year while waiting for our immigration approval. Moving to Canada was a transitional step before finally moving to the U.S., and it was important for us to help my brother and me learn English before the final move.

My family immigrated to the United States starting in 1997 when my dad was assigned to Hyundai in Ann Arbor, Michigan. After living in South Korea and Canada, we finally settled in the U.S. in 2008, moving to Statesboro, Georgia, and later to Newnan, Georgia. I graduated high school in Georgia and now attend the University of Georgia. Last April, my brother and I became American citizens, which was a major milestone for us.

I am currently a Mechanical Engineering student at the University of Georgia in Athens. I moved to Athens in 2012 after graduating high school in Newnan, Georgia. Attending this university has been an important part of my life as I continue my education and grow personally and academically.

My dad worked for Hyundai, which is why our family first moved to the United States in 1997. He was assigned to the Hyundai laboratory in Ann Arbor, Michigan, and this job influenced many of our moves. Hyundai played a big role in shaping our family's immigration journey and where we lived.

In 1997, my family moved to Ann Arbor, Michigan because of my dad’s job at the Hyundai laboratory. This was my first experience living in the United States, and it was a big adjustment for me as a timid child. I struggled with going to school and adapting to the new culture during that time.

In 2002, my family moved back to South Korea, specifically to Gyeonggi Province. This move was difficult because I had to leave my comfortable life and make new friends in a new environment. Although I spoke Korean, I found schoolwork harder and felt out of place, but over time I adjusted and made many friends.

We lived in London, Ontario, Canada for about a year starting in 2007 while waiting for our U.S. immigration to be finalized. This was a transitional period for my family, and it was important for my brother and me to learn English during this time. After our immigration was approved, we moved from London to Statesboro, Georgia.

In 2008, after our immigration was complete, my family moved to Statesboro, Georgia. It was hard at first because of the strong southern accent and the lack of Asian people in the community. I faced some bullying and misunderstandings, but I gradually got used to the new environment and culture.

In 2009, my family moved to Newnan, Georgia because of my dad’s job. I finished high school there in 2012 before moving on to college. Newnan was an important place for me during my teenage years and education.

After graduating high school in Newnan, I moved to Athens, Georgia in 2012 to attend the University of Georgia. Athens has been my home for college, where I am studying Mechanical Engineering and growing into adulthood. It’s where I am shaping my future and identity.
